deck: The first in a mini series on company culture: Jon Jackson, Amie Vaccaro, and four Dimagi team members unpack Be Bold But Humble, the first of Dimagi's newly articulated values, and why how the work gets done matters as much as the work itself.
seo_description: Dimagi unpacks Be Bold But Humble, the first of its newly articulated company values, with Lucina Tse, Gillian Javetski, Avni Singhal, and Simon Kelly.
og_description: Inside Dimagi's first articulated value, Be Bold But Humble, with four team members and co-hosts Jon and Amie.

pull_quote.text: At Dimagi we're not afraid of making mistakes. We're not afraid of failing. We actually like to fail, and learn through each other.

bullets: Why articulate values at all: making unspoken expectations explicit as Dimagi grows quickly around the world
Built to be controversial: the advice that good values should help some people realize Dimagi is not the place for them
From skeptic to proud: Jon Jackson on doubting the process for over a year, then seeing values that were genuinely unique to Dimagi
We fix the printer: the team nickname for not walking by problems, whoever they belong to
Scaling in India: how Lucina Tse and Simon Kelly rewrote the databases to go from thousands to hundreds of thousands of users
Bold but still learning: testing and prototyping the path forward rather than betting everything on one big leap of faith
Boldness with partners: why high aspirations require humility about how hard the work, and the partners' jobs, really are

stats.label: of failing and learning that Dimagi draws its humility from
newly articulated Dimagi values, of which Be Bold But Humble is the first
users the platform had to scale to, up from thousands, on the India project

guests.name: Lucina Tse
guests.role: Chief Operating Officer, with nearly a decade at Dimagi spanning the tech and operations teams.

guests.name: Gillian Javetski
guests.role: Chief of Staff who helped lead the values process, drawing on conversations with values experts on best practices.

guests.name: Avni Singhal
guests.role: Senior Customer Success Manager and a member of the team that crafted the new values.

guests.name: Simon Kelly
guests.role: Director of Server Engineering based in Cape Town, who helped draft the values and rewrote the backend architecture to scale the platform.
